details.body:
finding_summary: Historical lineage is resolved from the live milestone plan instead of the plan hash/version captured on the task/run.
severity: medium
affected_files: ["src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Use the persisted `plan_hash`/`plan_version` (or persist a bead-title/acceptance snapshot) when filling lineage metadata, and fall back to durable IDs instead of projecting today's plan onto old runs/tasks.

Evidence: `load_task_lineage_detail` always reparses the current `plan.json` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1364-1426`), and `read_bead_lineage` / `bead_execution_history` / `list_tasks_for_milestone` do the same (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2065-2218`). Those lookups ignore provenance already stored on `TaskSource` and `TaskRunEntry` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs:25-41`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:364-376`). After any milestone replan or bead rename/removal, these queries will silently relabel historical tasks/runs with the current bead title and acceptance criteria, or return empty lineage details for runs that were valid under the earlier plan version.

Bug: `load_task_lineage_detail` (project_run_record/service.rs:1364-1427) propagates errors from `milestone_store.read_milestone_record()` and `plan_store.read_plan_json()` via `?`. If a project has a `task_source` pointing to a milestone whose record or plan.json has been deleted or is corrupt, `show_project` returns an error — breaking `project show` and `task show` entirely for that project. Lineage is supplemental display data; it should gracefully degrade to `Ok(None)` when the milestone/plan cannot be loaded. Fix: catch errors from the milestone/plan loading path and return `Ok(None)` instead of propagating. Affected files: src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs (load_task_lineage_detail, lines 1377-1383).

Maintainability: `load_task_lineage_detail` (project_run_record/service.rs:1386-1418) reimplements the bead-in-plan lookup logic (workstream iteration, bead ID matching with milestone prefix stripping, acceptance criteria resolution from acceptance_map) that is already factored out as `resolve_bead_plan_details` + `build_bead_lineage_view` in milestone_record/service.rs:2077-2127, and exposed via the public `read_bead_lineage` function. The two implementations could diverge if matching rules change. Fix: replace the body of `load_task_lineage_detail` with a call to `crate::contexts::milestone_record::service::read_bead_lineage(milestone_store, plan_store, base_dir, &milestone_id, &task_source.bead_id)`, which already does the same work. This also simplifies adding the graceful degradation from the first amendment. Affected files: src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs (load_task_lineage_detail).

finding_summary: Non-milestone `run history --json` responses omit `milestone_id` and `bead_id` entirely instead of emitting explicit `null` values. Both fields are marked with `skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"` in [src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:292-297] and the CLI's JSON wrapper repeats the omission in [src/cli/run.rs:10764-10769]. That violates the stated backward-compat contract for standalone tasks and prevents JSON consumers from distinguishing "standalone task" from "older binary that never exposed lineage".
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:292-297
- src/cli/run.rs:10764-10769
remediation_hint: Keep the fields optional in Rust, but remove `skip_serializing_if` on the JSON-facing history structs and add a regression test that asserts `"milestone_id": null` / `"bead_id": null` for a non-milestone project.

finding_summary: Milestone-backed task detail queries lose all lineage when `plan.json` is missing or unreadable. `load_task_lineage_detail()` returns `None` as soon as `read_plan_json` or plan deserialization fails in [src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1363-1405], even though `read_bead_lineage()` already has a fallback that preserves milestone name/id and bead id without current-plan metadata in [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2131-2193]. As a result, `project show` / `task show` make a milestone-linked task indistinguishable from a standalone task after plan loss or corruption, which defeats the new lineage query.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1363-1405
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2131-2193
remediation_hint: Stop preemptively returning `None` on missing/corrupt plan artifacts; call `read_bead_lineage()` directly once the milestone exists and let its fallback path return milestone/bead lineage with empty title/acceptance details when plan metadata is unavailable.

finding_summary: Missing plan hashes are treated as "current plan", so historical milestone tasks/runs with unknown provenance can be relabeled with today's bead title and acceptance criteria.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2141-2169
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2224-2233
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2298-2313
- src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1379-1388
remediation_hint: Only project plan-derived fields when every referenced task/run carries an explicit plan hash that matches the current plan; if any hash is missing or mixed, fall back to milestone/bead IDs with bead_title/acceptance_criteria omitted.

finding_summary: The new lineage readers trust any JSON-shaped `plan.json`; they do not verify `bundle.identity.id` or run bundle validation, so a parseable but wrong/corrupt plan can feed bogus bead titles and acceptance criteria into task detail and milestone queries.
severity: low
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2065-2077
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2182-2188
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2285-2306
remediation_hint: Reuse the validated loader pattern already used in `src/cli/project.rs:812-840` (identity check, legacy backfill, `bundle.validate()`), and treat any validation failure like the existing missing/corrupt-plan path so the query degrades to fallback lineage instead of returning potentially incorrect metadata.

finding_summary: Bead execution history still filters task-run lineage rows with raw string equality, so `bead_execution_history("bead-1")` and `bead_execution_history("ms-alpha.bead-1")` are not equivalent even though milestone bead refs are canonicalized elsewhere.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/fs.rs:5161-5171
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2206-2224
- src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:3-15
remediation_hint: Canonicalize the requested bead ref and stored row ids (or compare via `milestone_bead_refs_match`) before filtering, and add a test that mixes short and fully qualified bead IDs.

finding_summary: `bead_execution_history` can report one execution attempt twice when the underlying start/completion rows use short vs fully-qualified bead IDs. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/adapters/fs.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs"]. evidence: the new query path filters rows with `milestone_bead_refs_match` in `FsTaskRunLineageStore::find_runs_for_bead` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:5168-5172`), but attempt collapsing still keys on raw `bead_id` equality because `TaskRunEntry::same_attempt` requires `left.bead_id == right.bead_id` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:393-405`) and `collapse_task_run_attempts` relies on that matcher (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:611-698`). If a running row was written as `bead-1` and the terminal replay/repair row was written as `ms-alpha.bead-1` for the same `project_id`/`run_id`, collapse leaves two entries and the new filter returns both, so retries/history become incorrect. remediation_hint: canonicalize bead refs before grouping, or make the collapse/matching helpers use milestone-aware bead comparison consistently.

finding_summary: task detail lookups now silently hide lineage whenever milestone lookup fails, so a broken bead-backed task becomes indistinguishable from a standalone project/task. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]. evidence: `load_task_lineage_detail` converts every `read_bead_lineage` error into `Ok(None)` at `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1379-1389`. But `read_bead_lineage` already downgrades missing/corrupt `plan.json` into a fallback lineage internally (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2230-2241`), so the remaining errors are real failures such as a missing/unreadable milestone record. In those cases `project show` / `task show` now succeed with no lineage at all, which defeats the new feature and masks on-disk corruption. remediation_hint: keep the internal plan fallback, but propagate hard milestone-store errors (or return an explicit degraded lineage state) instead of collapsing them to `None`.

finding_summary: Mixed short/qualified bead IDs are only normalized on the read side. `record_task_run_start` can discover a prior finalized attempt via canonicalized matching, but `reopen_failed_exact_attempt` still filters raw rows with `entry.bead_id == bead_id` at src/adapters/fs.rs:4541-4550, and `finalize_task_run_internal` does the same at src/adapters/fs.rs:4693-4699. If lineage stored `bead-1` and the current task uses `ms-alpha.bead-1` (or vice versa), retries and completion/repair syncs fail with "already finalized" or "no matching task run" even though the attempt was found. severity: high. affected_files: [src/adapters/fs.rs]. remediation_hint: Canonicalize bead refs in the raw-row reopen/finalize paths too, and add retry/complete tests that mix short and qualified bead IDs.

finding_summary: Snapshot reconciliation still treats short and qualified bead refs as different beads. `latest_task_runs_per_bead` keys by raw `entry.bead_id` at src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:554-568, and `reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage` uses that output for `active_bead` and progress counts at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:953-989. A retry history containing both `bead-1` and `ms-alpha.bead-1` for one logical bead will be double-counted, and two active mixed-form rows can even trip the "multiple active beads present in lineage" corruption path. severity: medium. affected_files: [src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs]. remediation_hint: Group latest runs by canonical milestone bead ref rather than the raw stored string.

finding_summary: `project show` / `task show` now fail outright when a bead-linked project's milestone record is missing. `show_project` unconditionally loads lineage at src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1363-1417, and `read_bead_lineage` returns `MilestoneNotFound` as soon as the milestone directory is absent at src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2222-2237. That turns an otherwise readable project into an error even though the project record, run snapshot, and raw milestone/bead IDs still exist. severity: medium. affected_files: [src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs]. remediation_hint: Degrade to `task_lineage: None` or a partial lineage stub when the milestone record is missing instead of aborting the whole detail query.

show_project (service.rs:1412-1417) propagates MilestoneNotFound as a hard error via `?` on load_task_lineage_detail. This means `project show` and `task show` become completely unusable for any task whose referenced milestone has been deleted or whose milestone_id is invalid. The test `show_project_propagates_missing_milestone_lineage_errors` confirms this is intentional, but it breaks the backward-compat guarantee: previously `show_project` succeeded for all existing projects regardless of milestone state. A project with task_source referencing a stale milestone should still be inspectable — the lineage should fall back to None (like when the plan is corrupt/missing) rather than failing the entire operation. Fix: in `load_task_lineage_detail`, catch `MilestoneNotFound` errors and return `Ok(None)` instead of propagating, matching the graceful fallback pattern already used for corrupt/missing plans.

finding_summary: `run history` still returns `milestone_id`/`bead_id` as null for milestone-backed projects created through the generic `create_project` path. The canonical project record persists `task_source` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1292-1300`), but `run_history` derives lineage only from journal events (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1560-1584`, `src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:347-371`). `create_project` does not copy `input.task_source` into the initial `project_created` event (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:302-313`), so the new history fields are wrong whenever the journal lacks the duplicated lineage metadata.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs
- src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs
remediation_hint: Treat `ProjectRecord.task_source` as the source of truth for history lineage, or at minimum inject `task_source` into the initial journal details whenever `create_project` receives it.

finding_summary: Task detail queries now silently drop lineage when the milestone record is missing. `load_task_lineage_detail` converts `MilestoneNotFound` into `None` (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1377-1387`), and the text renderers only print lineage when `task_lineage` is present (`src/cli/project.rs:772-786`, `src/cli/task.rs:149-163`). A milestone-backed task whose milestone directory was deleted or not checked out therefore becomes indistinguishable from a standalone task even though `ProjectRecord.task_source` still contains the milestone/bead IDs.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs
- src/cli/project.rs
- src/cli/task.rs
remediation_hint: Preserve a fallback lineage view from `task_source` (at least milestone_id/bead_id) and surface that the milestone metadata is missing instead of dropping lineage entirely.

finding_summary: The new milestone lineage queries accept unknown bead IDs as successful empty results instead of reporting an invalid lookup. `resolve_bead_plan_details` falls back to `(None, [])` when the bead is absent from the plan (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2120-2154`), and both `read_bead_lineage` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2222-2247`) and the no-runs branch of `bead_execution_history` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2279-2284`) return that fallback. A typo or stale bead ID is therefore indistinguishable from a real bead with no metadata/history.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs
remediation_hint: When the plan loads successfully and the requested bead is not present in it, return a not-found/corrupt-record error rather than an empty lineage view.

finding_summary: The new bead-alias matching only adds the milestone prefix when the bead id contains no dot, so short-form ids like `8.5.3` are treated as already-qualified and no longer match `9ni.8.5.3` [src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:8-15]. That bad canonicalization now feeds `TaskRunEntry::same_attempt()` / `canonical_task_run_bead_ref()` and `FsTaskRunLineageStore::find_runs_for_bead()`, so mixed short/qualified task-run rows for the same bead will not collapse together and `bead_execution_history()` can return duplicate or missing attempts for the dot-separated bead ids this repo actually uses [src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:452-472, src/adapters/fs.rs:5173-5183]. severity: high. affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs", "src/adapters/fs.rs"]. remediation_hint: canonicalize short dotted aliases against the current milestone instead of treating every dotted id as foreign/fully-qualified, and add coverage for a real alias pair such as `9ni.8.5.3` versus `8.5.3`.

finding_summary: `canonicalize_milestone_bead_ref` now treats every all-numeric dotted bead ref as a local short alias, which breaks matching when milestone IDs themselves are numeric.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/bead_refs.rs:8-23`
- `src/adapters/fs.rs:4371-4372`
- `src/adapters/fs.rs:4997-4998`
- `src/adapters/fs.rs:5180-5183`
remediation_hint: Do not blindly prefix every numeric dotted ref. Either prove the dotted alias belongs to the current milestone (for example from the current plan/bead graph) before canonicalizing, or narrow the heuristic so fully qualified foreign numeric milestone refs are preserved.

finding_summary: The new lineage queries silently swallow every `load_plan_bundle` failure and return fallback lineage instead of surfacing unexpected plan-store errors.
severity: low
affected_files:
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2247-2250`
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2286-2288`
- `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2352-2353`
remediation_hint: Keep the current fallback for expected stale-plan cases, but propagate unrelated `AppError`s such as filesystem/permission failures so callers can distinguish "plan unavailable/corrupt" from "the store is broken".
